cta.title: Begin Your Journey Toward Natural Well-Being
cta.subtitle: Starting with a consultation is one of the best decisions you can make. We'll take the time to understand your health goals, explore which treatments may be the right fit, and create a care plan built specifically around you.

faqs.answer: Most people find acupuncture quite comfortable and tolerate it very well. The needles themselves are hair-thin — significantly finer than those used for injections — so insertion usually produces nothing more than a brief, subtle sensation. Once the needles are in place, many patients settle into a deep sense of relaxation, and it's not at all unusual to fall asleep during the session.
faqs.question: Does acupuncture hurt?

faqs.answer: The number of sessions you'll need varies based on your condition, how long you've been experiencing it, and the way your body responds to treatment. Acute issues may begin to improve within 3–6 sessions, while chronic conditions often require 10–15 treatments or more. From your very first visit, we'll develop a personalized treatment plan and adjust it as we monitor how you're progressing.
faqs.question: How many treatment sessions will I need?

faqs.answer: In the hands of a qualified, licensed practitioner, Chinese medicine is regarded as safe. Every treatment uses sterile, single-use needles, and we follow strict safety protocols without exception. Chinese herbal medicine has been refined through thousands of years of clinical use, and we source only high-quality, tested herbs from trusted suppliers. Side effects are uncommon and, when they do occur, are generally minor.
faqs.question: Is Chinese medicine considered safe?

faqs.answer: Absolutely — TCM integrates well alongside Western medical care. Patients often seek acupuncture to help manage side effects from medications or procedures, to aid in recovery, or to address concerns that conventional treatments have not fully resolved. We always encourage you to keep all of your healthcare providers informed so your overall care stays coordinated and consistent.
faqs.question: Can TCM be used together with Western medical treatment?

faqs.answer: Comfortable, loose-fitting clothing is your best choice — opt for pieces that can be rolled up easily to allow access to your arms, legs, and back. Gowns are available should you need one. We also ask that you avoid wearing perfume or heavily scented products on treatment days, as some patients have sensitivities to strong fragrances.
faqs.question: What should I wear to my acupuncture appointment?

faqs.answer: We accept most major insurance plans that include acupuncture coverage. If your plan requires you to submit claims directly, we can prepare a detailed superbill for you to send to your insurer for potential reimbursement. We recommend reaching out before your visit to verify your specific benefits. For patients paying out of pocket, we offer straightforward self-pay rates and package pricing options.
faqs.question: Do you accept insurance at your practice?

faqs.answer: Eat a light meal one to two hours before your appointment — coming in on an empty stomach is not recommended. On the day of your treatment, try to keep alcohol and caffeine intake to a minimum. Wear comfortable clothing, and bring a list of any medications or supplements you're currently taking. Be ready to share your health history in some detail, and feel welcome to bring any questions you'd like to discuss.
faqs.question: How should I prepare for my first visit?

faqs.answer: Feeling nervous about needles is more common than you might expect, and many patients are pleasantly surprised by how gentle acupuncture actually is. The needles are extremely fine, and insertion is typically painless. We'll guide you through every step at a pace that feels comfortable, and we're happy to start with fewer needles if that helps put you at ease. Needle-free alternatives are also available, including herbal medicine, cupping, and Tui Na massage.
faqs.question: What if I'm nervous about needles?

faq.title: Answers to Your Most Common Questions
faq.subtitle: Clear, straightforward information about our services, safety standards, and what to expect from your care

hero.title: Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) Treatment Modalities
hero.subtitle: Comprehensive healing therapies, carefully tailored to meet your unique health needs

overview.title: Areas Our Care Model Addresses
benefits: Natural support for managing stress and anxiety
Support for healthy immune system function
Improved energy levels and a refreshed sense of vitality
Digestive wellness and gut health support
Support through injury recovery and the rehabilitation process
overview.introduction: At Dr. Henry Smith Clinic, we offer a comprehensive range of Traditional Chinese Medicine treatments, each selected and adapted with your specific health concerns and wellness goals in mind. By weaving together time-honored practices and contemporary clinical understanding, we provide natural, whole-person care that looks beyond symptoms to address the underlying root of your concerns.

items.title: Licensed Practitioners
items.description: Treatment rooted in evidence-informed clinical practice

items.title: Personalized Treatment Plans
items.description: Your care plan is built around your individual health needs and personal goals

items.title: Safe & Professionally Delivered
items.description: Consistent adherence to high hygiene standards and professional care protocols

items.title: Acupuncture
benefits: Encourages balanced, healthy immune system function
Eases feelings of stress and anxiety
items.whatToExpect: Once you're settled comfortably, your practitioner carefully places hair-thin needles at specific points on your body. Most patients experience little to no discomfort during this process. You then rest quietly for 20 to 30 minutes while the needles work — and a deep, restful calm often sets in. Many patients find themselves drifting off to sleep before the session is over.
items.fullDescription:
Through the precise placement of needles along the body's meridians, acupuncture supports the balance of Yin and Yang and promotes the healthy flow of Qi and blood. 

**Scope of Application**
Acute and chronic pain conditions (including neck, shoulder, back, and leg pain, headaches, and arthritis), internal medicine concerns (such as insomnia, gastrointestinal disorders, and hypertension), surgical and soft tissue issues (including soft tissue injuries and skin conditions), gynecological concerns (such as irregular menstruation and menopausal syndrome), pediatric conditions (including indigestion and low immunity), men's health (including prostate enlargement, impotence, premature ejaculation, and sexual dysfunction), and aesthetic TCM applications (including acne management, anti-aging, weight management, skin lifting, and edema reduction). 

**Precautions**
Avoid coming to your appointment on an empty stomach or right after a heavy meal. Those who are pregnant or managing severe heart disease or bleeding disorders should take extra care. Be sure to let your acupuncturist know if you take any anti-platelet or anticoagulant medications. After your session, keep warm, stay away from cold drafts, and wait at least 4 hours before bathing.
items.shortDescription: At the heart of our practice, acupuncture uses fine needles to help restore the body's natural equilibrium and support its innate capacity for healing.

items.title: Chinese Herbal Medicine
benefits: Personalized to your specific health needs and constitution
Addresses root causes rather than masking surface-level symptoms
Typically well tolerated, with a low side-effect profile
Well suited to supporting the management of ongoing and chronic conditions
Supports and strengthens immune system resilience
Helps support healthy internal organ function over time
Promotes sustained wellbeing over the long term
items.whatToExpect: We start with a thorough consultation and TCM diagnosis to understand your health picture fully. A herbal formula is then crafted around your individual needs, and you'll receive clear instructions on how to prepare and take your herbs at home. As your treatment continues, we check in with you regularly and adjust the formula as needed to keep your progress on track.
items.fullDescription:
Drawing on TCM syndrome differentiation, Chinese Herbal Medicine uses thoughtfully selected herbal formulas to harmonize the Yin, Yang, Qi, and blood of the organs. 

**Scope of Application**
Internal medicine conditions (including stomach complaints, liver disease, and cardiovascular disease), gynecological concerns (such as menstrual disorders, infertility, and menopause), pediatric issues (including colds and indigestion), men's health (including kidney deficiency and impotence), recovery from chronic illness, and anti-aging and wellness support. 

**Precautions**
Herbal formulas should always be taken under the supervision of a qualified TCM practitioner. If you are currently using Western medications, please tell your practitioner so the herbal prescription can be tailored appropriately. Pregnant women and those with special health circumstances should follow their practitioner's guidance closely.
items.shortDescription: Custom herbal formulas crafted to address the underlying causes of your health concerns and work with your body's own healing process.

items.title: Cupping Therapy
benefits: Encourages healthy blood flow and circulation
Aids the body's recovery process following injury
items.whatToExpect: Glass or silicone cups are placed over areas of tension or discomfort on your body. Rather than feeling painful, most people describe the suction as a satisfying, pulling pressure. Cups may remain stationary for 5 to 15 minutes or be gently glided across the skin. It's normal to see temporary circular marks afterward — these are completely harmless and usually fade within a few days.
items.fullDescription:
By applying negative pressure to draw cups against the skin, cupping therapy expands the underlying capillaries to promote blood flow, clear stagnation, and support the body in expelling wind and cold. 

**Scope of Application**
Colds, coughs, asthma, rheumatic joint pain, and muscle tension in the neck, shoulders, and back arising from wind-cold pathogens. 

**Precautions**
Cupping should not be applied over broken or irritated skin, and is not suitable for those with bleeding disorders or over the abdominal and lower back areas of pregnant women. Elderly patients should proceed with extra care. After your session, protect yourself from cold exposure, avoid overexertion, and wait before bathing to prevent wind-cold from entering through the pores.
items.shortDescription: A well-established therapy that uses suction cups to release muscle tension, improve circulation, and help the body along its natural path to recovery.

items.title: Moxibustion
benefits: Nourishes and supports healthy immune function
Supports the restoration of energy levels and a sense of vitality
Supports healthy and comfortable digestive function
items.whatToExpect: During your moxibustion session, burning moxa is held close to targeted points on your body, releasing a gentle, penetrating warmth. The experience is grounding and often deeply relaxing — patients frequently compare the sensation to the soft warmth of sunlight on their skin. The moxa herb also carries a distinctive, earthy aroma that many find soothing.
items.fullDescription:
Moxibustion uses the therapeutic heat produced by burning moxa leaves to activate specific acupuncture points, warm the meridians, and nourish the body's vital energy. 

**Scope of Application**
Deficiency-cold constitutions, menstrual cramps, chronic gastrointestinal conditions, rheumatic pain, asthma, and general fatigue or cold extremities. 

**Precautions**
Care must always be taken to avoid burns during treatment. Moxibustion is not appropriate for individuals with high fever or constitutions characterized by excess heat. Those with diabetic neuropathy should exercise extreme caution. Pregnant women should avoid application to the abdominal and sacral regions.
items.shortDescription: A soothing warmth-based therapy using dried mugwort to nourish vital energy, support healthy circulation, and help the body dispel cold.

items.title: Tui Na Medical Massage
benefits: Relieves muscle pain and persistent tension
Supports improved posture and better structural alignment
May support a reduction in the frequency and intensity of headaches and migraines
items.whatToExpect: Tui Na can range from gentle and calming to more active and stimulating, depending on your condition and constitution. Your practitioner uses a variety of techniques applied through their hands, fingers, elbows, and occasionally feet. You remain fully clothed throughout the session, which typically lasts between 30 and 60 minutes.
items.fullDescription:
Using skilled manual techniques applied to the body's meridians, muscles, and joints, Tui Na works to harmonize Qi and blood, release tension held in the tendons, and improve circulation in targeted areas. 

**Scope of Application**
Neck, shoulder, back, and leg pain, muscle strain, sports injuries, indigestion, and pediatric conditions such as cough or constipation. 

**Precautions**
Tui Na should not be applied over open wounds, active skin infections, or areas affected by bone tumors. Deep abdominal pressure is not appropriate for pregnant women. When working with elderly patients or those with osteoporosis, only gentle techniques should be used.
items.shortDescription: A traditional Chinese medical massage that weaves together acupressure, assisted stretching, and joint mobilization into a single, cohesive therapeutic experience.

items.title: Gua Sha
benefits: May help the body recover from illness more comfortably
Easing of tension held in the neck and shoulders
items.whatToExpect: To begin, your practitioner applies oil to the treatment area, then draws a smooth-edged tool across the skin using firm, downward strokes to address tension and discomfort. The pressure is noticeable and the scraping sensation can feel quite strong, though most people find it very tolerable. It is common to see redness or purplish marks appear on the skin during treatment — these typically fade on their own within 2 to 7 days.
items.fullDescription:
Gua Sha uses a smooth tool applied with controlled friction to encourage circulation, clear the meridians, and bring stagnant blood to the surface so the body can release pathogens more effectively. 

**Scope of Application**
This therapy is frequently used to address colds, fever, sore throat, heatstroke, hypertension, and tightness or stiffness in the neck, shoulders, and back. 

**Precautions**
Avoid applying Gua Sha over broken skin or moles. It is not suitable for individuals with bleeding disorders, and should not be performed on the abdomen during pregnancy. Following treatment, keep the area covered and warm, and take care to avoid cold drafts or bathing immediately afterward.
items.shortDescription: A traditional scraping therapy that works to relieve muscle tension, encourage healthy circulation, and help calm inflammation.

items.title: Herbal Plaster Therapy
items.fullDescription:
Specially prepared herbal pastes or powders are placed directly on targeted acupoints or areas of pain, enabling the medicinal properties of the herbs to absorb through the skin where they are needed most. 

**Scope of Application**
This approach is commonly applied for rheumatic joint pain, ongoing waist and leg pain, asthma, rhinitis, pediatric cough conditions (including San-Fu-Tie), and various skin concerns. 

**Precautions**
Use care when applying to areas of sensitive skin, and avoid placing plasters on broken skin or open wounds. Should notable redness, swelling, or itching occur, remove the plaster and discontinue use promptly.
items.shortDescription: Personalized nutritional guidance rooted in TCM principles, crafted to support your body's healing process and help bring it back into balance.

items.title: Lifestyle & Wellness Counseling
benefits: Meaningful, enduring improvements to your health
Actionable techniques for handling everyday stress
Improved energy levels and an enhanced sense of vitality
Proactive steps to help lower the likelihood of future illness
Stronger overall outcomes from your treatment plan
Individualized approaches to support your long-term wellbeing
items.whatToExpect: During your care, we take a thoughtful look at different aspects of your daily life and share clear, practical guidance you can begin applying right away. Topics may include breathing exercises, meditation, healthy sleep habits, adjusting your routines across the seasons, and effective ways to manage stress. Throughout this process, our focus is on working with you to build changes that feel both achievable and sustainable over time.
items.fullDescription:
True, lasting wellbeing reaches beyond what happens in the treatment room.

Our lifestyle counseling takes a broad view of your health — exploring your sleep quality, physical activity levels, how you manage stress, and the habits that shape your day-to-day life.

Every suggestion is grounded in TCM principles and carefully adapted to reflect your unique constitution and personal situation.
items.shortDescription: Holistic guidance that addresses sleep, movement, stress, and daily habits — all in service of your continued health and wellness.
